markdown:
### Comparison: Original Transformer vs. BERT

| Dimension | Original Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) | BERT (Devlin et al., 2018) |
|---|---|---|
| **(1) Architecture role** | **Encoder-decoder.** A stack of N=6 encoder layers plus a stack of N=6 decoder layers; the decoder adds a third sub-layer performing multi-head attention over the encoder stack's output, used for sequence transduction (machine translation). | **Encoder-only.** "Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers": stacks Transformer encoder layers only to produce contextual representations for downstream tasks; there is no decoder. |
| **(2) Directionality / attention masking** | **Auto-regressive / causal in the decoder.** Decoder self-attention masks out (sets to -inf before softmax) all positions to the right of position i, so each position depends only on known outputs at positions < i. Encoder self-attention is unmasked, but the model as a whole is left-to-right. | **Fully bidirectional (no causal mask).** Self-attention has no left/right restriction; every token attends to the entire input sequence, fusing left and right context. |
| **(3) Pre-training / training objective** | **Supervised training from scratch on labeled translation data** (WMT 2014 EN-DE / EN-FR) with a standard sequence-to-sequence cross-entropy loss over target tokens. No pre-training stage; the Transformer is the architecture that BERT later builds on. | **Self-supervised pre-training on unlabeled text** with two objectives: (a) **Masked Language Modeling (MLM)** - predict the original vocabulary id of randomly masked tokens from full bidirectional context; (b) **Next Sentence Prediction (NSP)** - binary IsNext/NotNext classification of sentence pairs. The pre-trained encoder is then fine-tuned on downstream tasks. |

**Key takeaway.** BERT keeps the Transformer's encoder block but drops the decoder, removes the decoder's causal mask to enable true bidirectionality, and replaces supervised translation training with two self-supervised pre-training objectives (MLM + NSP) explicitly designed to overcome the unidirectionality imposed by the original Transformer's decoder.
summary: 3x2 comparison of the original Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) and BERT (Devlin et al., 2018) along architecture role, attention directionality/masking, and training/pretraining objective, grounded in primary-source evidence spans from both papers.
